contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: truesight.me Beer Hall feed — jsDelivr primary + raw.github fallback (GFW + reliability fix)
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/82
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_prod/pull/1
  
  Details:
  Gary reported the Recent ecosystem updates section on https://truesight.me/ was empty. Diagnosis: a headless Chromium render from this machine showed the section populating correctly with the latest digests, so the data and JS were fine on this network. The most likely user-side symptom matched a GFW pattern — raw.githubusercontent.com is blocked in mainland China and occasionally rate-limits aggressive refreshes elsewhere.
  
  Fix: rewrote js/ecosystem-change-log-feed.js to try jsDelivr first (cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/TrueSightDAO/ecosystem_change_logs@main/) and fall back to raw.githubusercontent.com on any error. Both paths serve byte-identical content from the same Git tree. jsDelivr has aggressive edge caching (~12h on @main, acceptable since Beer Hall digests are weekly-ish) and reaches into China via Alicloud + Cloudflare. RAW_BASE kept as a historical alias of FALLBACK_BASE so any caller referencing it directly keeps working.
  
  Shipped to both repos because truesight.me is deployed from truesight_me_prod, while truesight_me_beta is the canonical dev fork:
  - TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta#82 squash-merged 6da214a
  - TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_prod#1 squash-merged b036180
  
  Post-deploy verification (headless Chromium on https://truesight.me/, 90s after the prod merge):
  - ecosystem-updates-root populated with 5 items
  - first item title 'field signals 4 5 autopilot self healing' dated May 14 2026 (the latest digest)
  - window.TrueSightEcosystemFeed.PRIMARY_BASE includes 'jsdelivr' confirmed live
  
  This is a companion to the project_dapp_routes_migration.md theme — client-side resilience for GFW users. The DApp side has Edgar /proxy/gas/:name; this is the equivalent dual-CDN pattern for static read-only feeds where adding a server hop would be overkill.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
